conditions.text: Consider a synchronous generator connected to an infinite bus by two identical parallel transmission lines as shown in the image.
The transient reactance 'x' of the generator is 0.1 pu, and the mechanical power input to it is constant at 1.0 pu.
Due to some previous disturbance, the rotor angle (\(\delta\)) is undergoing an undamped oscillation, with the maximum value of \(\delta(t)\) equal to \(130^\circ\).
One of the parallel lines trips due to relay maloperation at an instant when \(\delta(t) = 130^\circ\) as shown in the image.

targets.text: Consider a synchronous generator connected to an infinite bus by two identical parallel transmission lines as shown in the image. Due to some previous disturbance, the rotor angle (\(\delta\)) is undergoing an undamped oscillation, with the maximum value of \(\delta(t)\) equal to \(130^\circ\). One of the parallel lines trips due to relay maloperation at an instant when \(\delta(t) = 130^\circ\) as shown in the image. The maximum value of the per unit line reactance, x, such that the system does not lose synchronism subsequent to this tripping is ________

answer_slots.target_text: What is the maximum value of the per unit line reactance such that the system does not lose synchronism subsequent to this tripping?

conditions.text: A three-phase slip-ring induction motor, provided with a commutator winding, is shown in the figure.
The motor rotates in a clockwise direction when the rotor windings are closed.

If the rotor winding is open-circuited and the system is made to run at rotational speed \( f_r \) with the help of a prime mover in an anti-clockwise direction, then the frequency of voltage across slip rings is \( f_1 \) and the frequency of voltage across commutator brushes is \( f_2 \).
The values of \( f_1 \) and \( f_2 \), respectively, are ______.
